title: A market in another country I'd love to visit
summary: Describe an imagined visit to a market in a foreign country.
topic: A market in another country I'd love to visit
prompt: Write 150–250 words describing a market in another country that you would love to visit. Imagine the colours, smells, and sounds. Use present simple for the description and 'would like / would love' for your wishes.
hintStarters: One day, I would love to visit…
I imagine the air smells of…
I think I would walk slowly because…
sampleText: One day, I would love to visit the Jemaa el-Fnaa market in Marrakech, Morocco. I have only seen it in photos and short videos, but it lives in my head like a place I already know. The narrow streets are full of bright colours: orange spices, red carpets, blue plates, and yellow lemons. I imagine the air smells of mint tea, fresh bread, and a little smoke. Sellers call out from every corner. Their voices are loud, but their smiles are friendly. I think I would walk slowly because there is too much to see in one minute. I would stop at a spice stall and ask the price of saffron. I am not good at bargaining, but I would try. The seller would smile and offer me a small glass of mint tea. I would sit on a low stool and drink it slowly while he wrapped my spices in brown paper. After that, I would climb to a rooftop café and watch the market from above. The afternoon sun would be soft on the buildings, and the call to prayer would float over the square. I do not need to buy anything special. I just want to be there for one full day, and to remember it with my eyes closed for many years.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Would love to' for wishes
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'would love to + động từ nguyên thể' để diễn tả mong ước lịch sự: 'I would love to visit the Jemaa el-Fnaa market.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'would love to + base verb' for a polite wish: 'I would love to visit the Jemaa el-Fnaa market.'

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for description
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả nơi chốn ở dạng tưởng tượng, dùng present simple: 'The narrow streets are full of bright colours.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When describing a place (even imagined), use present simple: 'The narrow streets are full of bright colours.'

sampleTranslationVi: Một ngày nào đó, tôi rất muốn ghé thăm chợ Jemaa el-Fnaa ở Marrakech, Maroc. Tôi mới chỉ thấy nó qua ảnh và video ngắn, nhưng nó sống trong đầu tôi như một nơi tôi đã biết. Những con phố hẹp đầy màu sắc tươi sáng: gia vị màu cam, thảm đỏ, đĩa xanh, và chanh vàng. Tôi tưởng tượng không khí có mùi trà bạc hà, bánh mì mới và một chút khói. Người bán hàng gọi mời từ khắp các góc. Giọng họ to nhưng nụ cười thân thiện. Tôi nghĩ mình sẽ đi chậm vì có quá nhiều thứ để xem trong một phút. Tôi sẽ dừng ở một quầy gia vị và hỏi giá nghệ tây. Tôi không giỏi mặc cả, nhưng tôi sẽ thử. Người bán sẽ mỉm cười và mời tôi một ly nhỏ trà bạc hà. Tôi sẽ ngồi trên một chiếc ghế thấp và uống chậm rãi trong khi anh ấy gói gia vị vào giấy nâu. Sau đó, tôi sẽ trèo lên một quán cà phê trên mái nhà và ngắm khu chợ từ trên cao. Nắng chiều sẽ nhẹ trên các tòa nhà, và tiếng cầu nguyện sẽ trôi trên quảng trường. Tôi không cần mua gì đặc biệt. Tôi chỉ muốn ở đó một ngày trọn vẹn, và nhớ về nó với đôi mắt nhắm trong nhiều năm.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'would love to'?
options: I would love visit the Jemaa el-Fnaa market.
I would love to visit the Jemaa el-Fnaa market.
I would love to visiting the Jemaa el-Fnaa market.
I would loved to visit the Jemaa el-Fnaa market.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would love to' is followed by the base verb.

options: The narrow streets are full of brightly colours.
The narrow streets are fulled of bright colours.
The narrow streets are full of bright colours.
The narrow streets is full with bright colours.
mcQuestions.explanation: Plural subject takes 'are', and 'full of' is a fixed phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Subject-verb agreement and the fixed phrase 'full of'.

options: The air smells like of mint tea and fresh bread.
The air smells from mint tea and fresh bread.
The air is smelling of mint teas and fresh bread.
The air smells of mint tea and fresh bread.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smell of + noun' is the natural collocation.

options: I would walk slowly because there is too much to see.
I would walk slowly so there is too much to see.
I would walk slowly but there is too much to see.
I would walk slowly because there are too much to see.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because' introduces a reason; 'there is + uncountable' is correct.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correctly formed conditional-style sentence.
options: I am not good in bargaining, but I would try.
I am not good at bargaining, but I would try.
I am not good at bargain, but I would tries.
I am not good at bargaining, but I will trying.
After this, I would climbed to a rooftop café.
After that, I would climbing to a rooftop café.
After that, I would climb to a rooftop café.
After this, I will climb in a rooftop café.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses the right adjective form?
options: Their voices are loudly, but their smiles are friend.
Their voices are loud, but their smiles are friendlier.
Their voices loud are, but their smiles friendly are.
Their voices are loud, but their smiles are friendly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Loud' and 'friendly' are adjectives; 'loudly' is an adverb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural way to express a polite wish.
options: I just want to be there for one full day.
I just am wanting to be there for one full day.
I just want being there for one full day.
I just want to am there for one full day.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Want + to + base verb' is the standard pattern.

options: The afternoon sun would been soft on the buildings.
The afternoon sun would be soft on the buildings.
The afternoon sun would soft be on the buildings.
The afternoon sun is would be soft on the buildings.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Would' is followed by the base form.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which closing line avoids AI clichés?
options: In conclusion, markets are an important part of culture.
Throughout history, markets have connected people.
I just want to be there for one full day.
It is important to note that travel broadens the mind.
mcQuestions.explanation: A personal, concrete wish beats generic AI phrasing.
mcQuestions.hint: Skip 'In conclusion', 'Throughout history', or 'It is important to note'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If you could only visit one place in another country for a single day, would you choose a famous landmark or an ordinary market? Why?
